mission.title: oras: Can't download an OCI package twice if package contains symbolic links
mission.description: Can't download an OCI package twice if package contains symbolic links. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify oras troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your oras deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras -n oras --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review oras configuration
steps.description: Inspect the relevant oras configuration: ```bash kubectl get all -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras kubectl get configmap -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oras ``` ### What happened in your environment? 1) Created a package containing a symbolic link: 2) Pushed it to my OCI complaint

steps.title: Apply the fix for Can't download an OCI package twice if package contains…
steps.description: Bump the `oras-go` dependency to the latest commit [`cb6d75be7dd4a78f25fe9d00c76c0d15c4296da6`](https://github.com/oras-project/oras-go/commit/cb6d75be7dd4a78f25fe9d00c76c0d15c4296da6) which includes the bug fix for https://github.com/oras-project/oras-go/issues/865. *(optional, in `fixes # 4) Without cleaning anything in the output folder, I tried the same command from (3) again, and then it failed: ```

steps.title: Confirm Can't download an OCI package twice if package… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras -n oras --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n oras --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Bump the `oras-go` dependency to the latest commit [`cb6d75be7dd4a78f25fe9d00c76c0d15c4296da6`](https://github.com/oras-project/oras-go/commit/cb6d75be7dd4a78f25fe9d00c76c0d15c4296da6) which includes the bug fix for https://github.com/oras-project/oras-go/issues/865.

*(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*:
codeSnippets: 4) Without cleaning anything in the output folder, I tried the same command from (3) again, and then it failed:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oras installed or the issue environment reproducible.
